desc: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.
goal: Perform a statistical analysis of sales volumes, revenue contributions, and price sensitivity across different products or product categories. This descriptive approach identifies key revenue drivers and top-selling items, using visual tools such as bar charts and pie charts to highlight performance differences, which in turn supports decisions related to pricing strategies, inventory management, and promotional activities.
name: Visual Analytics System blinkit_goal3_1742643898

effect.description: Selecting a product category will filter data in subsequent views to show only products within that category.

visualization.description: A bar chart displaying the total sales volumes for each product category. Categories are shown on the x-axis, with sales volumes on the y-axis, and each category is visually distinguished by color.

effect.description: Clicking on a slice triggers the breakdown of data for that specific category in subsequent views.

visualization.description: A pie chart showing the percentage revenue contributions of each product category. Each slice corresponds to a category, with size proportional to the total revenue from that category.

views.components: A slider below the scatter plot allows for filtering data based on unit price ranges.

effect.description: Displays details like unit price, sales volume, and revenue contribution for the hovered product.
Filters the scatter plot to show only points (products) within the selected price range.

visualization.description: A scatter plot of unit price vs. sales volume, where bubble size represents total revenue contribution. Tooltip provides details for each product.

views.components: Temporal selectors (dropdown menus) to switch between viewing trends monthly, quarterly, or yearly.

effect.description: Displays detailed revenue metrics for the hovered time point.

visualization.description: A line chart showing temporal trends of sales volume and revenue, grouped by product category. Tooltip provides details on total revenue per category for a selected time period.

effect.description: Filters data in earlier views to focus on the clicked category and time period.

visualization.description: A heatmap visualizing category performance across months. The intensity of cell colors represents total revenue.

data.reason: The error indicates that the 'month' function is not recognized, likely because the database system requires a different approach to extract the month from a timestamp.
data.requirement: Ensure local time is handled correctly when computing month from order_date in all SQL queries to avoid function errors.

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW sales_volume_by_category AS SELECT blinkit_products.category, sum(blinkit_order_items.quantity) AS total_sales FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY blinkit_products.category;
sqls.name: sales_volume_by_category

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW revenue_by_category AS SELECT blinkit_products.category, sum(blinkit_order_items.quantity * blinkit_order_items.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY blinkit_products.category;
sqls.name: revenue_by_category

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW unit_price_vs_quantity AS SELECT blinkit_order_items.unit_price, sum(blinkit_order_items.quantity) AS total_quantity, sum(blinkit_order_items.quantity * blinkit_order_items.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM blinkit_order_items GROUP BY blinkit_order_items.unit_price;
sqls.name: unit_price_vs_quantity

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW sales_trend_by_date AS SELECT blinkit_orders.order_date, sum(blinkit_order_items.quantity * blinkit_order_items.unit_price) AS total_revenue, blinkit_products.category FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_orders ON blinkit_order_items.order_id = blinkit_orders.order_id JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY blinkit_orders.order_date, blinkit_products.category;
sqls.name: sales_trend_by_date

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW category_performance_heatmap AS SELECT month(blinkit_orders.order_date) AS order_month, blinkit_products.category, sum(blinkit_order_items.quantity * blinkit_order_items.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_orders ON blinkit_order_items.order_id = blinkit_orders.order_id JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY month(blinkit_orders.order_date), blinkit_products.category;
sqls.name: category_performance_heatmap

tasks.method: Aggregate total sales volumes and revenue contributions for each product and product category by summing quantity and calculating revenue (quantity x unit price) using group-by operations. Visualize this data in a bar chart for categories (V1) and a pie chart for percentage revenue contributions (V2).
tasks.objective: Identify top-performing products and categories in terms of sales volumes and revenue to drive inventory and pricing decisions.
tasks.type: aggregating values | sorting | characterizing distributions | finding extrema

tasks.method: Correlate unit prices of products with their sales volumes. Create a scatter plot with unit price on the x-axis and sales volume on the y-axis to assess price sensitivity of individual products (V3). Include a range slider for unit prices to allow interactive exploration of the relationship (I3).
tasks.objective: Analyze price sensitivity to understand how changes in unit prices affect demand for individual products.

tasks.method: Characterize the distribution of sales volumes and revenues across products and categories. Use box plots to highlight variability, outliers, and top-performing products/categories. Supplement with a bar chart to rank categories based on total revenue (V1).
tasks.objective: Spot outliers and identify clusters of top-selling or underperforming products and categories.

tasks.method: Aggregate sales volumes and revenues over time by grouping data by order_date (monthly, quarterly, yearly). Visualize using a line chart with time on the x-axis and metrics (sales volume/revenue) on the y-axis (V4). Enable temporal selectors (e.g., monthly, yearly) for insights into seasonal trends or recent performance (I5).
tasks.objective: Understand temporal trends to identify seasonality or evaluate the effectiveness of promotional activities on sales and revenue.
tasks.type: aggregating values | determining ranges | plotting temporal trends

tasks.method: Combine product category-level data with monthly aggregates of sales volume and revenue. Represent the results in a heat map with categories on the y-axis, months on the x-axis, and intensity showing sales volume/revenue (V5).
tasks.objective: Highlight category performance trends and seasonal variability across months to pinpoint focus areas for promotions or inventory stocking.
tasks.type: computing derived values | aggregating values | visualizing patterns

tasks.method: Enable interactivity across all visualizations: add category or product-level filtering in bar charts, pie charts, and scatter plots (I1). Implement click interactions on pie charts to focus other visualizations on selected entities (I2). Provide hover interactions to display detailed metrics like unit price, revenue, and sales volumes in bar charts and scatter plots (I4).
tasks.objective: Enhance user engagement and ability to investigate specific products, categories, and metrics for detailed comparative analysis.

tasks.method: Calculate product revenue drivers by combining unit price, quantity, and product category. Cluster products based on their revenue contribution, unit price, and sales volume. Use a bubble chart to represent clusters, where bubble size correlates with revenue contribution.
tasks.objective: Cluster products to visually distinguish different revenue-driving groups and optimize inventory or promotional activities around these clusters.

tasks.method: Identify anomalies in sales performance by detecting drastic drops in revenue or sharp increases in sales volume relative to historical trends. Overlay anomalies on the line chart used for temporal trends (V4).
tasks.objective: Spot unexpected changes in product/category performance to inform urgent corrective actions or capitalize on trends.
